note: Very dark coins on bright background, strong bluish noise. Seed 0 shows clear numeral 5 + globe (5cent common face). Seeds 1 and 2 are unreadable (surface fully noise-masked); labels are from relative size vs the confirmed 5cent (r 65 and 71 vs 82): seed1/seed0=0.79 ~ 1cent, seed2/seed0=0.87 ~ 2cent. Copper-family color consistent for all three.

Single coin, no in-image scale reference. After denoise+CLAHE a large numeral ending in 0 is visible on the right half with a thin leading digit, most consistent with 10; 20cent is the alternative. Coin badly degraded with bluish cast.

Fabric background. Seeds 0 and 1 show clear 5 + globe; seed 4 clear 1 + globe; seed 5 clear 2 + CENT. Seed 2 unreadable but radius 66.8 matches confirmed 1cent (68.9). Seed 3 unreadable, radius 77.9 sits between confirmed 2cent (74.8) and 5cent (~84): ratio to 5cent = 0.93 matches 10cent (19.75/21.25) and its a-channel is the least red of all coins, mildly supporting gold family; worn 5cent is the alternative.
Clear 1 + CENT + globe (1cent common face) despite bluish degradation. Circle well fitted.

Single coin, surface almost entirely noise-masked. Faint concentric elliptical arcs in the upper half resemble the Colosseum (Italian 5cent national side), hence the 5cent guess; genuinely could be another copper denomination. No usable scale reference across images.
Numeral 20 visible (diagonal 2 + 0) with vertical stripes of the old common face, and the edge shows the scalloped Spanish-flower indentations distinctive of the 20cent.
